kick_title: Cycle 16 — HEXA-WEAVE W5 path mismatch fix — tool/hexa_weave_w5_setup.hexa Step 6/8 HW_SCRIPTS_DIR patch (1.0.0 -> 1.0.1)

options_evaluated.A_user_cp: rejected: two-source-of-truth drift on every script update
options_evaluated.B_env_override: SELECTED: single-source-of-truth + raw 142 D2 minimal-scope + raw 47 cross-repo explicit
options_evaluated.C_relative_resolver: rejected: runtime probe complexity for marginal benefit

improvements_bundled: --resume flag added to Step 6 (raw 65 idempotent + raw 142 D2)
test -f probe with actionable error message (raw 66 ai-native)
raw 47 cross-repo edge declaration in caveats
summary.selftest_evidence: YAML parse PASS (schema=hexa/runbook/v1 version=1.0.1 total_steps=8); bash -n PASS (rc=0); HW_SCRIPTS_DIR resolves to ~/core/canon/scripts; rcsb_cluster_split_fetch.py + w5_verdict.py both --help-able; CLI flag conformance 100% (--out --train --val --test --seed --resume for Step 6; --pred --ref --vram-csv --rmsd-max --tm-min --vram-max-mb --out for Step 8)
summary.user_dispatch_readiness: READY (1-command dispatch from ~/core/canon/ on ubu1; HW_SCRIPTS_DIR env override available for arbitrary script locations)

raw_91_c3_honest.cycle_14_prep_mistake_acknowledged: cycle 14 authored runbook before cycle 15 authored scripts; implicit assumption was sister-repo location. Neither cycle individually wrong; mismatch is cross-cycle handoff bug, closed cycle 16.
raw_91_c3_honest.option_a_vs_b_evaluation: Option A (user mkdir+cp) is slightly less invasive but creates two-source-of-truth that drifts whenever a script is updated. Option B (env override) preserves single-source-of-truth at cost of one extra shell variable + one extra test -f line per Step. Option C (auto-detect resolver) deferred as runtime-probe-complex.
raw_91_c3_honest.sister_repo_still_required: cycle 16 does NOT collapse repos; sister hexa-weave/ holds large data (3 GB weights, 100 FASTAs, 50 MB outputs) inappropriate for source repo.
raw_91_c3_honest.hexa_parse_noise_preexisting: hexa parse emits 60+ errors on the .hexa runbook YAML; this is pre-existing cycle-14 baseline and not caused by cycle 16. The .hexa-suffixed runbook is YAML-spec, not strand-typed hexa-lang. Implied by cycle-14 caveat #1.
raw_91_c3_honest.no_user_dispatch_yet: cycle 16 closes the path mismatch but does NOT execute W5; user dispatch on ubu1 is still pending (raw 71 paper-publication-tier human gate).
raw_91_c3_honest.vram_ladder_path_unaffected: tool/hexa_weave_vram_ladder.hexa (cycle 15 219 LoC) does NOT reference scripts/ — uses raw nvidia-smi only — so no path mismatch fix needed there.

selftest_evidence_inline: YAML parse PASS; bash -n PASS; HW_SCRIPTS_DIR live probe PASS (PROBE-1 + PROBE-2 PASS with actual file existence); --help dry-run PASS for both scripts; CLI flag conformance 100% across argparse-defined signatures.

user_decision_items: user_decision_1: Approve cycle 16 patch as 1-command dispatch path; default HW_SCRIPTS_DIR=$HOME/core/canon/scripts implies user has cloned canon next to hexa-weave (~/core/canon + ~/core/hexa-weave). Confirm.
user_decision_2: Optional override for users on different filesystem layouts; document in W5 dispatch instructions whether to advertise HW_SCRIPTS_DIR=... prefix or rely on default.
user_decision_3: Defer cycle 17+ sister-repo README HW_SCRIPTS_DIR mention OR fold into cycle 16 (mention only — README spec already authored cycle 15 as proposal, not committed to disk yet).
